State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

June 6, 1985


Clark H. Alsop
Deputy County Counsel
County of San Bernardino
County Government Center
385 North Arrowhead Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0140

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-85-081

Dear Mr. Alsop:

I apologize for any inconvenience our delay in answering
your letter may have caused you. In response to your questions,
I enclose a copy of an advice letter to Donald Fallon of the
Santa Clara County Counsel's Office (A-85-050) also sent this
date.

If you have further questions, please feel free to contact
me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Diane Maule Fishburn
Counsel
Legal Division

DMF:plh
Enclosure

ALAN K. MARKS
County Counsel

ROGER N. KEHEW JR.
Assistant County Counsel

EUGENE L. HOLDER
CRAIG S. JORDAN
Chief Deputies County Counsel

Deputies County Counsel
E. H. Robinson
Paul A. Grube, Jr.
Charles A. Duerbeck
Clark H. Alsop
Dawn Stafford
Ronald D. Reitz
Charles J. Larkin
Paul F. Mordy
Daniel B. Haueter
Ruth E. Stringer
Charles L. Eddleman

Legal Research Attorney
Jonathan Weg

COUNTY COUNSEL

County Government Center
385 North Arrowhead Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0140
(714) 383-2761

April 3, 1985

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, CA  95814

Attention:  Ms. Barbara A. Milman, Chief
            Legal Division

RE:  Chapter 1681 (AB 2992),
     Statutes of 1984

Dear Ms. Milman:

     This letter is sent to request that you inform us as to the
Fair Political Practices Commission's determination as to
whether or not all of the powers of a Local Agency Formation
Commission are subject to the requirements of Government Code
§ 84308 as amended by Chapter 1681 (AB 2992), Statutes of 1984.
This concern arises from the fact that while a Local Agency
Formation Commission is an "Agency" as that term is used in
Government Code § 84308, the Agency's decisions rarely involve
the issuance of a "License, permit or other entitlement for
use" as that term is used in Government Code § 84308.

     Government Code § 84308(a)(5) defines "License, permit or other
entitlement for use" as follows:

          (5)  "License, permit, or other entitlement for
     use" means all business, professional, trade and
     land use licenses and permits and all other enti-
     tlements for use, including all entitlements
     for land use, all contracts (other than compet-
     itively bid, labor, or personal employment con-
     tracts), and all franchises.

Ms. Barbara A. Milman                -2-                    April 3, 1985


Government Code § 54790(a)(3) provides in pertinent part
that:

     "A commission shall not impose any conditions
     which would directly regulate land use, property
     development or subdivision requirements."
     Emphasis added.

LAFCO does not issue any business, professional or trade licenses
or permits.  Similarly, LAFCO does not issue franchises.  LAFCO
does not issue "entitlements for use" as that term is commonly
understood because formations, incorporations, annexations and
other changes of organization approved by LAFCO must be acted
upon by a conducting authority which is another legislative
body, subject to various public protest provisions and elections
requirements.  We believe that LAFCO may enter certain contracts
which are subject to the requirements of Government Code § 84308.

Your response to our request will provide assistance to us as
well as others who have been concerned with the status of Local
Agency Formation Commissions under § 84308.  Thank you for your
assistance in this matter.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        ALAN K. MARKS
                                        County Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        CLARK H. ALSOP
                                        Deputy County Counsel

CHA:md

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-6441

April 15, 1985


Clark H. Alsop
Deputy County Counsel
County Government Center
385 North Arrowhead Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92415-0140

Re: A-85-081

Dear Mr. Alsop:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act has been referred to Diane Fishburn, an attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or unless more information is needed to answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel

BAM;plh

sections.question: whether or not all of the powers of a Local Agency Formation Commission are subject to the requirements of Government Code § 84308 as amended by Chapter 1681 (AB 2992), Statutes of 1984, particularly given that LAFCO's decisions rarely involve the issuance of a 'License, permit or other entitlement for use' as defined in Government Code § 84308(a)(5)
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO) powers are subject to Government Code § 84308 requirements, given that LAFCO decisions do not typically involve issuance of licenses, permits, or entitlements for use as defined in that statute.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC response indicates that the substantive advice letter would be provided separately (referencing a parallel advice letter A-85-050 to Donald Fallon), but the specific conclusion is not included in the transmitted document.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (April 3, 1985) and FPPC's acknowledgment/referral response (April 15, 1985), plus a subsequent response letter (June 6, 1985) referencing an enclosure. The actual substantive advice conclusion is not present in this document; it references enclosure A-85-050. Confidence reduced due to incomplete conclusion section.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: whether or not all of the powers of a Local Agency Formation Commission are subject to the requirements of Government Code § 84308 as amended by Chapter 1681 (AB 2992), Statutes of 1984, particularly given that LAFCO's decisions rarely involve the issuance of a 'License, permit or other entitlement for use' as defined in Government Code § 84308(a)(5)

CONCLUSION: The FPPC response indicates that the substantive advice letter would be provided separately (referencing a parallel advice letter A-85-050 to Donald Fallon), but the specific conclusion is not included in the transmitted document.

embedding.summary: San Bernardino County Counsel's Office requested FPPC advice on whether Local Agency Formation Commission powers are subject to Government Code § 84308 requirements, particularly regarding licenses, permits, and entitlements for use. FPPC responded by referring the matter to an attorney and indicating that substantive advice would follow.
